evolution_report.notes: Refresh after PRs #1-#4. Several init-era facts about hardcoded clientId, worker.destS3Client being unwired, and HEAD-then-GET hot path are superseded. The new accesslog package adds a cluster of observability facts.

facts.statement: Service is a single-binary Go HTTP server with 6 internal packages: cmd/image-proxy (main), internal/types (interfaces), internal/s3, internal/resizer, internal/accesslog (NEW since init), internal/server, internal/worker.
Dependency injection is constructor-based via internal/types interfaces (Resizer, S3Client). No DI container.

Server holds two S3Client references: originClient (where the upstream catalog system writes originals) and cacheClient (where the proxy writes resized variants). In CacheMode=off they are the same instance.
CacheMode is an exported enum (Off, Shadow, Live) with ParseCacheMode. Off is the no-op default; Shadow dual-writes with reads from origin; Live dual-writes with reads from cache.
Hot-path single speculative GET against effective read client. NoSuchKey/NotFound is a clean miss → fall through to regex matching. No prior HEAD-then-GET pair.
effectiveReadClient selects the cache-hit read target from (mode, X-Use-Cache header). header 'true' forces cache; 'false' forces origin; any other value defers to mode default.

putBoth is the dual-write helper. In Off mode: single Put on cacheClient (= originClient) under bare phase 's3-put'. In Shadow: origin first under 's3-put-origin' then cache under 's3-put-cache'. In Live: cache first then origin. Each Put is best-effort; per-side failure is logged and the other side is still attempted.

Fallback bucket is consulted only on origin-side miss in s3.Client.Get and s3.Client.Exists; the cache client never has a fallback. Hits on the fallback are copied to the primary origin bucket (lazy migration), no tags.
Fallback retry strategy: try original key, then key with the leading <clientId>/ prefix stripped. No deeper alternates.
Three URL families: resizeRegex (resize products/blocks/branding), fileRegex (passthrough file), folderImageRegex (format-change / passthrough image).

POST /_/worker/trigger accepts a JSON envelope: {clientId, version, images, sizes, formats}. clientId/images/formats are required; sizes/version are optional. Validation runs synchronously before the 202 — any failure returns 400 with a descriptive body naming the failing field.
X-Use-Cache request header overrides the cache-hit read source for one request. Value 'true' → cache; 'false' → origin; absent/other → mode default. No-op when CacheMode=Off. Writes are not affected.
Resized output is always cached back to S3 (via putBoth) under the canonical normalized key before being served.
Cache-Control header is max-age=31536000 on 2xx image responses; max-age=30 on errors via httpError.
Requests whose filename portion has no '.' are rejected with 404 before any S3 lookup.
BUCKET env var is required; main.go calls log.Fatal if absent.
CACHE_MODE != off requires CACHE_BUCKET; main.go calls log.Fatal otherwise. CACHE_MODE = off with CACHE_BUCKET set logs a warning and ignores the cache config.
vips.Startup is called once at process start; vips.Shutdown via defer from main. Every vips.ImageRef is Close()'d via defer in Resize.

POST /_/worker/trigger dispatches a detached goroutine using context.Background and returns 202 immediately. The HTTP response does not reflect the resize outcome. Validation is synchronous; the goroutine never starts on validation failure.
Production code has one named lock: accesslog.Timings.mu (per-request, mutex-guarded phase accumulator). No sync.Mutex elsewhere; no shared mutable state across requests post-startup.
Production deps: aws-sdk-go-v2 (s3, config, credentials, eventstream, service/s3/types), govips/v2 (libvips). CGO required. No new modules added since init.

All runtime configuration is via process env vars, evaluated once in main.go. Surface grew from 14 to 22 vars since init (added CACHE_MODE, CACHE_BUCKET, CACHE_S3_ENDPOINT, CACHE_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, CACHE_AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, CACHE_AWS_REGION). IMAGE_TAGS is deprecated (logged-and-ignored at startup).
OLD_S3_BUCKET enables an origin-side fallback bucket via originClient.SetFallback. The cache client never has a fallback by construction. OLD_S3_REGION defaults to AWS_REGION when unset.

Test mocks use struct-of-function-pointer pattern (e.g. mockS3Client.existsFunc/getFunc/putFunc); no mocking library. Worker tests additionally have mockResizer.Calls() counter + failOnCall hook for failure-isolation testing.
Each test package has TestMain that silences log output unless DEBUG=true env is set.
os.Getenv is called only in cmd/image-proxy/main.go; internal packages receive configuration as constructor arguments.

Not-found detection uses AWS SDK v2 typed errors (*types.NoSuchKey, *types.NotFound) via errors.As, with a string-match fallback for HOS-style providers that don't surface typed errors directly. isNotFound helper in s3.go and isNotFoundErr helper in server.go both implement this pattern.

POST /_/worker/trigger has no authentication; relies on load balancer / network controls. After PR #4 removed the hardcoded clientId=13, the impersonation surface is any tenant prefix on both origin and cache buckets.
s3.NewClient logs the AccessKey ID when using static credentials; the secret is never logged.
types.Storage interface is declared but is not implemented or referenced anywhere — dead code at this snapshot. Unchanged since init.

accesslog.Middleware wraps the *Server in main.go. Per request it: resolves correlationId (X-Request-ID > CF-Ray > 32-hex random), echoes it in X-Request-ID response header, installs a *Timings on context, wraps the response writer for status+bytes capture, and emits a single JSON log line to stdout after the inner handler returns.

The JSON access log Entry has fixed top-level field order: @timestamp, extra, user, request, response, upstream, timings. The 'timings' key is always present (empty {} on no-phase requests). New top-level fields should be appended at the end for log-parser prefix stability.

Server-Timing response header is set on the FIRST WriteHeader call from the *Timings recorded up to that point. Phases recorded after the body is written remain in the access log Entry's timings map but cannot appear in the header (already on the wire).

Server-Timing header uses milliseconds with 1 decimal; the JSON log 'timings' map uses seconds with 3 decimals via round3 (same source-of-truth, different consumer conventions). upstream.responseTime is the sum of timings within float epsilon.

Phase names used by s.time(ctx, name, fn) in server.go: s3-get (cache check + candidate-key GETs against origin), resize (libvips), s3-put (off-mode only, bare), s3-put-cache + s3-put-origin (shadow/live).

Worker.ProcessBatch fans out the (images × sizes × formats) cartesian product. Each image is Get'd from originClient ONCE and resized once per (size, format) output. Per-image Get failure skips the image; per-output Resize failure skips the output. Batch never returns an error.
Worker's exists-check targets destS3Client when set (cache in split mode), not s3Client. Skipping a pre-warm output when the cache already has it is the correct behavior.

s3.Client.Put no longer accepts a tags parameter. PutObjectInput.Tagging is never set anywhere in the codebase. IMAGE_TAGS env var is logged-and-ignored at startup; HOS doesn't implement S3 Tagging, R2 rejects it.

Storage topology in production today: Hetzner Object Storage on the origin side. Cloudflare R2 canary planned via CACHE_MODE shadow → live transition, with the X-Use-Cache: true header used for synthetic-monitor read-perf comparison during the shadow phase.

make test (Alpine Docker, via docker compose run --rm tester) is the source-of-truth test command — it has libvips/pkg-config installed. Host `go test` works for libvips-free packages: types, s3, server, worker, accesslog.
